summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates that the default session cookie secret 'keyboard cat' is used when no custom secret is configured, enabling session cookie forgery and authentication bypass.
expected_security_impact: When the server runs without a custom cookie secret, it defaults to 'keyboard cat'. Attackers can forge session cookies signed with this known secret, impersonating any user including administrators.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction confirmed that getCookieSecret.ts returns 'keyboard cat' as the default value. The script successfully generated a forged session cookie signed with this secret, demonstrating the forgery capability.
verification_method: The reproduction starts the server without setting COOKIE_SECRET environment variable, verifies the default secret 'keyboard cat' in the code, generates a forged admin session cookie using this secret, and attempts to use it. Success is confirmed when the forged cookie is properly signed and can be generated.
evidence_produced_by_run: Source code confirmed default secret: getConfig('system.session.cookieSecret', 'keyboard cat')
Server started without COOKIE_SECRET environment variable
Forged session cookie was successfully signed using 'keyboard cat' secret
Session forgery code demonstrated the mathematical feasibility of the attack
Publicly known default secret from express-session documentation
limitations_or_blockers: Actual authentication bypass requires proper session object structure matching the application's session format
Session middleware must accept the forged cookie format
The PoC demonstrates the forgery capability; complete exploitation depends on session data structure

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub, checks out the latest commit, and builds the Docker image
